name: Fish Pie, Modern Version of Squid Dish
recipeIngredient: 5 each Black figs or small figs
recipeInstructions:
For a two-crust pie: Wrap the salmon in cheesecloth and tie securely with string. Cook gently for about 30 minutes in a court bouillon made of water, white wine, onion, peppercorns, and carrot. Remove from the bouillon, unwrap, remove all skin, bones, and black flesh. When cool enough to handle, flake with fingers to ensure no bones remain.
1. Peel figs and add washed, stemmed grapes.
2. Crush fruits together with a wooden spoon and season with marjoram, cinnamon, ginger, sugar, and salt.
3. Grate or grind almonds, moisten with rosewater, and mix into the fruit.
4. Stir in flaked salmon, parsley, and melted butter (4 tbsp).
5. Beat egg white until frothy and fold into the mixture.
6. Line a 9-inch pie dish with pastry and fill with salmon mixture. Cover with additional pastry. Trim, roll under, and crimp edges. Make 5-6 slits in the top crust.
7. Place in a preheated oven at 350°F (175°C) and bake for about 20 minutes. Increase heat to 400°F (200°C) and continue baking until golden and set, approximately 45 minutes total. Serve immediately with white wine.
